We calculate the sum of elements in both parts.

- 1 if Sum(Decreasing) > Sum(Increasing)

The reference solution uses binary search:
Initialize $L=0, R=n-1$.
While $L < R$:
  $M = (L+R)/2$
  If $nums[M] < nums[M+1]$ (increasing slope), the peak is to the right, so $L = M+1$.
  Else ($nums[M] > nums[M+1]$, decreasing or at peak), the peak is at $M$ or to the left, so $R = M$.

### 2. Calculating Sums

Once `peakIndex` is found:
SumInc = sum(nums[0]...nums[peakIndex])
SumDec = sum(nums[peakIndex]...nums[n-1])

val n = nums.length
